name: adjacent_entry_overlap
code:
proof fn phi_1_adjacent_entry_overlap(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i + 1 < arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i + 1) < arch.next_entry_base(layer, base, i),
{
}
reason: Adjacent entries overlapping would mean two page table entries map the same virtual address, destroying isolation.

name: upper_vaddr_leq_base
code:
proof fn phi_2_upper_vaddr_leq_base(d: Directory)
    requires
        d.inv(),
    ensures
        d.upper_vaddr() <= d.base_vaddr,
{
}
reason: A directory whose upper bound does not exceed its base covers zero or negative address space, making it useless.

name: update_mutates_layer
code:
proof fn phi_3_update_mutates_layer(d: Directory, n: nat, e: NodeEntry)
    requires
        d.inv(),
        n < d.entries.len(),
    ensures
        d.update(n, e).layer != d.layer,
{
}
reason: If updating a single entry changes the directory's layer, the tree structure is silently corrupted.

name: entry_base_zero_idx_not_base
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_base_zero_idx_not_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, 0) != base,
{
}
reason: The first entry (index 0) not starting at the directory's base address would create an unmapped hole at the start of every directory.

name: entry_size_layer_inversion
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_size_layer_inversion(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(i) < arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat),
{
}
reason: A higher (deeper) layer having larger entry sizes than its parent would invert the page table hierarchy, making subdivision meaningless.
